rulings.Case Title: DENNIS MITCHELL VS. TIBURON TAXI, A BUSINESS ENTITY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 29, 2011, line 1. DEFENDANT GREG MCCORD MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant's motion for summary judgment/summary adjudication is denied as to the 1st cause of action. Defendant has provided enough evidence that Carroll was an independent to shift the burden of proof to plaintiff to show that Carroll was not. Plaintiff has presented considerable evidence to show that Carroll may not have been an independent contractor but defendant's employee. Therefore, triable issues of fact remain on the Borello factors. The defendant's motion for summary judgment/summary adjudication is denied as to the 2nd cause of action as well. Defendant claims that plaintiff has suffered no damages and therefore has no standing to bring this claim. Plaintiff, however, has shown that he has suffered at least $500 in damage for the deductible he had to pay to his insurance company and plaintiff's prayer included a prayer for injunctive relief as well. Defendant's motion for summary adjudication as to punitive damages is off calendar as moot. That claim was dismissed on August 24, 2011. The request for sanctions is denied. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: NICHOLAS WALKER et al VS. PLATINUM CAPITAL GROUP et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 29, 2011, line 3. DEFENDANT AURORA LOAN SERVICES DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrers to the 1st cause of action (set aside of Trustee Sale) and 5th cause of action (cancel Trustee's deed) are sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff has not opposed the demurrer on those claims. Defendant's demurrer to the 2nd cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff's claim that MER is not properly registered and failed to comply with California's tax laws is without merit factually and legally. Perlas v. MERS 2010 WL 3079262. Defendant's demurrer to the 3rd, 4th, 6th and 7th causes of action (fraud, negligent misrepresentation, violation of U.C.L. and Quiet Title) are sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead further facts with sufficient specificity to support these claims. Defendant's demurrer to the 8th cause of action (conspiracy) is sustained with leave to amend. Defendant properly cites Allied Equipment for the proposition that conspiracy is not a distinct cause of action. Plaintiff should re-plead the conspiracy allegations as a general charging allegation. Defendant's demurrer to the 9th cause of action (unjust enrichment) is sustained without leave to amend. Defendant properly cites McBride for the proposition that it is not a distinct cause of action. The demurrer to the 10th cause of action (TRO/injunctive relief). A request for a TRO is a remedy, not a cause of action. Because the prayer for relief seeks a TRO, this cause of action is surplusage. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID W. GUERDAT VS. SALLY A. UNTERBERGER et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 29, 2011, line 7. DEFENDANT SALLY UNTERBERGER DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer to the 1st cause of action (partition) is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead facts that he complied with the contracted agreement in the T.I.C.A. Defendant's demurrer to the 4th cause of action (declaratory relief) is overruled. An actual controversy exists . Defendant's demurrer to the 5th cause of action (waste) is overruled. Plaintiff has pled facts to support this claim. Defendant's demurrer to the 7th cause of action (indemnity) is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead actual losses that have accrued for the maintenance of the property. However, indemnity cannot be used as to losses not realized from the lawsuit. =(302/LMG)
